 {"id":"jeffreysprompts.com-94r0","title":"Add search integration for personal prompts in CLI","description":"Add search integration for personal prompts in CLI.

When user is authenticated with premium account, extend search to include:
- User's own prompts (if they have any)
- User's saved prompts
- User's collections (search collection names and descriptions)
- User's prompt notes

Search Flow:
1. Search local prompt registry first (fast, always available)
2. If authenticated, concurrently search premium API endpoints
3. Merge results, marking personal prompts with [personal] tag
4. Display combined results with source indicators

CLI Options:
- jfp search <query> --mine (search only personal prompts)
- jfp search <query> --saved (search only saved prompts)
- jfp search <query> --all (search everything, default for premium users)
- jfp search <query> --local (search only local registry, default for free users)

Graceful Degradation:
- If API unavailable, fall back to local-only search with warning
- If not authenticated, only search local registry (no warning)

 {"id":"jeffreysprompts.com-9566","title":"EPIC: Community Prompts Marketplace (Swap Meet)","description":"## Origin
Moved from jeffreysprompts_premium jeffreysprompts_premium-wox.

## Overview
The "Swap Meet" is the community marketplace where premium users can browse, discover, and share prompts with other users. This transforms JeffreysPrompts from a static prompt library into a living, user-driven ecosystem.

## Core Concept
- Premium users can make their own prompts PUBLIC (shared to community)
- By default, all user-created content is PRIVATE (only visible to creator)
- Public prompts appear in the Swap Meet for all premium users to discover
- Users earn reputation and visibility when their prompts get used/rated

## Key Features

### Browse & Discover
- Paginated gallery of community prompts
- Filter by category, tags, rating, recency
- Sort by: newest, most popular, highest rated, trending
- Featured prompts section (editor's picks)
- "Prompt of the day" highlight

### Search Community
- Full-text search across community prompts
- Search by title, description, content, tags
- Autocomplete suggestions
- Search within categories

### Prompt Detail View
- Full prompt content with syntax highlighting
- Author info with reputation badge
- Usage stats (saves, uses, ratings)
- Related prompts suggestions
- One-click save to library
- Copy to clipboard

### Submit to Community
- Toggle prompt visibility: Private → Public
- Add categories and tags
- Preview how it will appear
- Agree to community guidelines
- Edit/update published prompts
- Withdraw from community (make private again)

### Quality Signals
- User ratings (integrated with ratings epic)
- Usage statistics
- Author reputation
- Verified/featured badges

## Database Tables Needed
- community_prompts (or extend user_prompts with visibility)
- prompt_categories
- prompt_tags
- prompt_usage_stats
- featured_prompts

## Success Metrics
- Number of public prompts
- Browse/search conversion rate
- Saves per prompt
- Return visitors to Swap Meet

## Dependencies
- User authentication system
- User-created prompts functionality
- Ratings system
- Premium tier enforcement

-{"id":"jeffreysprompts.com-96v3","title":"Implement contact form and support ticket system","description":"## Origin
Moved from jeffreysprompts_premium jeffreysprompts_premium-uz41.

## Task
Implement a contact form and basic support ticket system for user inquiries.

## Why This Matters
- Users need a way to reach support
- Structured tickets are easier to manage
- Required for any paid product
- Builds trust and credibility

## Features

### 1. Contact Form
- Name, email, subject, message
- Category dropdown (billing, technical, feedback, other)
- Priority indication
- Attachment support (optional)
- Captcha/spam protection

### 2. Ticket Creation
- Auto-create ticket from form submission
- Send confirmation email to user
- Notify support team (email/Slack)
- Unique ticket ID

### 3. Ticket Categories
- Billing & Payments
- Technical Issue
- Feature Request
- Bug Report
- Account Help
- Other

### 4. Basic Ticket Management (Admin)
- View all tickets
- Filter by status/category
- Respond to ticket
- Close ticket
- Internal notes

### 5. User Ticket View
- "My Tickets" in settings
- View ticket history
- Reply to open tickets
- See status updates

## Integration Options
- **Simple**: Email-based (form sends email, reply via email)
- **Better**: Lightweight ticketing (Crisp, Intercom, Zendesk)
- **DIY**: Build simple ticket table

## Recommended: Start Simple
1. Contact form → email to support@
2. Reply from email client
3. Graduate to proper system if volume grows

## Database (if DIY)
```typescript
export const supportTickets = pgTable("support_tickets", {
  id: uuid("id").primaryKey().defaultRandom(),
  ticketNumber: text("ticket_number").notNull().unique(),
  userId: uuid("user_id").references(() => userProfiles.id),
  email: text("email").notNull(),
  category: text("category").notNull(),
  subject: text("subject").notNull(),
  message: text("message").notNull(),
  status: text("status").default("open"), // open, pending, resolved, closed
  priority: text("priority").default("normal"),
  createdAt: timestamp("created_at").defaultNow(),
  updatedAt: timestamp("updated_at").defaultNow(),
});
```

## Files to Create
- src/app/(public)/contact/page.tsx
- src/app/api/support/tickets/route.ts
- src/app/(app)/settings/tickets/page.tsx
- src/app/(app)/admin/tickets/page.tsx
- src/components/support/contact-form.tsx

## Success Criteria
- Form submits successfully
- Tickets tracked
- Users can view their tickets
- Support team notified

## Test Coverage
- E2E: jeffreysprompts_premium-xwg2 (contact + tickets)
- Unit: support ticket schema validation + spam checks

 {"id":"jeffreysprompts.com-rhvk","title":"E2E Tests: Network Resilience and Offline Mode","description":"## Origin
Mirror from jeffreysprompts_premium jeffreysprompts_premium-2b44. Keep both repos in sync.

## Task
Create E2E tests for network failure handling and offline mode.

## Why This Matters
- Users have bad connections
- Mobile users go offline
- Must handle gracefully
- No data loss

## Test Scenarios

### 1. Network Failure Handling
```typescript
test('should show error message on network failure', async ({ page, context }) => {
  await page.goto('/dashboard');
  
  // Simulate offline
  await context.setOffline(true);
  
  // Try to load more content
  await page.getByRole('button', { name: 'Load More' }).click();
  
  // Should show offline message
  await expect(page.getByText(/network.*error|offline/i)).toBeVisible();
});

test('should retry failed requests', async ({ page, context }) => {
  await page.goto('/dashboard');
  
  // Intercept and fail first request
  let requestCount = 0;
  await page.route('**/api/prompts**', async (route) => {
    requestCount++;
    if (requestCount === 1) {
      await route.abort('connectionfailed');
    } else {
      await route.continue();
    }
  });
  
  // Should auto-retry and succeed
  await page.reload();
  await expect(page.getByTestId('prompt-list')).toBeVisible();
  expect(requestCount).toBe(2);
});
```

### 2. Offline Indicator
```typescript
test('should show offline indicator when disconnected', async ({ page, context }) => {
  await page.goto('/dashboard');
  
  await context.setOffline(true);
  
  // Should show offline indicator
  await expect(page.getByTestId('offline-indicator')).toBeVisible();
  
  await context.setOffline(false);
  
  // Should hide indicator
  await expect(page.getByTestId('offline-indicator')).not.toBeVisible();
});
```

### 3. Offline Data Access (PWA)
```typescript
test('should serve cached prompts when offline', async ({ page, context }) => {
  // First, visit while online to cache
  await page.goto('/prompts/test-prompt-id');
  await expect(page.getByTestId('prompt-content')).toBeVisible();
  
  // Go offline
  await context.setOffline(true);
  
  // Should still show cached content
  await page.reload();
  await expect(page.getByTestId('prompt-content')).toBeVisible();
  await expect(page.getByText(/offline.*cached/i)).toBeVisible();
});

test('should queue actions when offline', async ({ page, context }) => {
  await page.goto('/prompts/test-prompt-id/edit');
  
  await context.setOffline(true);
  
  // Make an edit
  await page.getByLabel('Title').fill('Updated Title');
  await page.getByRole('button', { name: 'Save' }).click();
  
  // Should show "queued" message
  await expect(page.getByText(/saved.*sync/i)).toBeVisible();
  
  // Go back online
  await context.setOffline(false);
  
  // Should sync automatically
  await expect(page.getByText(/synced/i)).toBeVisible({ timeout: 10000 });
});
```

### 4. Slow Network Handling
```typescript
test('should show loading states on slow network', async ({ page }) => {
  // Simulate slow network
  const client = await page.context().newCDPSession(page);
  await client.send('Network.emulateNetworkConditions', {
    offline: false,
    downloadThroughput: 50 * 1024, // 50kb/s
    uploadThroughput: 50 * 1024,
    latency: 2000, // 2s latency
  });
  
  await page.goto('/search');
  await page.getByPlaceholder('Search').fill('test');
  await page.keyboard.press('Enter');
  
  // Should show loading skeleton
  await expect(page.getByTestId('search-skeleton')).toBeVisible();
});

test('should timeout and show message on very slow network', async ({ page }) => {
  // Simulate extremely slow network
  await page.route('**/api/**', async (route) => {
    await new Promise(resolve => setTimeout(resolve, 30000));
    await route.continue();
  });
  
  await page.goto('/dashboard');
  
  // Should show timeout message
  await expect(page.getByText(/taking.*long|timeout/i)).toBeVisible({ timeout: 15000 });
});
```

### 5. Reconnection Handling
```typescript
test('should automatically reconnect and refresh', async ({ page, context }) => {
  await page.goto('/dashboard');
  
  await context.setOffline(true);
  await page.waitForTimeout(1000);
  await context.setOffline(false);
  
  // Should show reconnection message
  await expect(page.getByText(/back online|reconnected/i)).toBeVisible();
  
  // Should refresh data
  await expect(page.getByTestId('last-updated')).toContainText(/just now/i);
});
```

## Logging Requirements
- Log network state changes
- Log retry attempts
- Log cache hits/misses
- Screenshot error states

## Files to Create
- e2e/network/failures.spec.ts
- e2e/network/offline.spec.ts
- e2e/network/slow.spec.ts
- e2e/network/reconnection.spec.ts

## Success Criteria
- Errors shown clearly
- Retries work
- Offline mode functions
- Reconnection smooth

 {"id":"jeffreysprompts.com-ri69","title":"Fix touch target sizes to meet 44px minimum","description":"# Task: Fix Touch Target Sizes

## Context
Apple and Google HIG recommend minimum 44x44px touch targets for comfortable interaction. Several components violate this guideline.

## Specific Violations

### 1. SpotlightSearch Category Pills (lines 517-543)
**Current:** `py-1.5` ≈ 28px height
```tsx
<button className="shrink-0 px-3 py-1.5 rounded-full text-xs font-medium ...">
```

**Fix:** Increase to `py-2.5` for ~40px, or add min-height
```tsx
<button className="shrink-0 px-3 py-2.5 min-h-[44px] rounded-full text-sm font-medium ...">
```

### 2. BasketSidebar Remove Button (lines 223-229)
**Current:** `h-7 w-7` = 28px
```tsx
<Button variant="ghost" size="icon" className="h-7 w-7 shrink-0">
  <X className="h-3 w-3" />
</Button>
```

**Fix:** Increase to `h-9 w-9` = 36px (or h-11 w-11 = 44px)
```tsx
<Button 
  variant="ghost" 
  size="icon" 
  className="h-9 w-9 shrink-0 -mr-2" // Negative margin to maintain visual spacing
  aria-label={`Remove ${prompt.title} from basket`}
>
  <X className="h-4 w-4" />
</Button>
```

### 3. SpotlightSearch Close Button (mobile, lines 497-504)
**Current:** `size-8` = 32px
```tsx
<button className="shrink-0 size-8 rounded-lg ...">
```

**Fix:** Already close, bump to `size-10` or `size-11`
```tsx
<button className="shrink-0 size-11 rounded-lg ...">
```

### 4. SpotlightSearch Semantic Toggle (lines 480-494)
**Current:** `size-8` = 32px
**Fix:** Bump to `size-10` for touch friendliness

## Pattern for Touch-Friendly Buttons
```tsx
// Utility class for consistent touch targets
// Could add to globals.css:
.touch-target {
  @apply min-h-[44px] min-w-[44px];
}

// Or use Tailwind directly:
className="min-h-[44px] min-w-[44px] ..."
```

## Design Considerations
- Larger touch targets may require layout adjustments
- Use negative margins to maintain visual density if needed
- Icon size should scale proportionally (but not required)
- Consider adding `touch-manipulation` class for faster response

## Visual Impact
- SpotlightSearch category pills will be slightly taller
- Basket remove buttons will have more breathing room
- Overall feel: more native/mobile-friendly

## Testing
1. Test on actual mobile device (simulator isn't accurate for touch)
2. Verify tap accuracy on category pills
3. Check remove button in basket with full list
4. Ensure no layout overflow issues
5. Compare before/after screenshots

## Files Modified
- apps/web/src/components/SpotlightSearch.tsx
- apps/web/src/components/BasketSidebar.tsx

## Dependencies
- Independent of color changes
- May affect overall mobile layout slightly","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-12T00:40:03.618658128-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-12T00:40:03.618658128-05:00"}

### Privacy Signals
- GPC/DNT defaults to analytics off
- Settings link accessible from footer

## Files to Create
- e2e/privacy/consent-banner.spec.ts
- e2e/privacy/consent-settings.spec.ts
- e2e/lib/consent-helpers.ts

## Success Criteria
- Consent UX works end-to-end
- Analytics gated correctly
- Privacy signals respected

 {"id":"jeffreysprompts.com-rnm","title":"Implement useLocalStorage hook","description":"**Details:**
- Generic hook for localStorage persistence
- SSR-safe (handles hydration)
- Type-safe with generics
- Used by: basket, theme, variable inputs, preferences

**Implementation:**
```typescript
// apps/web/src/hooks/use-local-storage.ts
'use client';

export function useLocalStorage<T>(key: string, initialValue: T) {
  // State to store our value
  const [storedValue, setStoredValue] = useState<T>(() => {
    if (typeof window === 'undefined') return initialValue;
    try {
      const item = localStorage.getItem(key);
      return item ? JSON.parse(item) : initialValue;
    } catch {
      return initialValue;
    }
  });

  // Persist to localStorage on change
  useEffect(() => {
    if (typeof window === 'undefined') return;
    try {
      localStorage.setItem(key, JSON.stringify(storedValue));
    } catch (error) {
      console.warn('Failed to save to localStorage:', error);
    }
  }, [key, storedValue]);

  return [storedValue, setStoredValue] as const;
}
```

**SSR Handling:**
- Initial render uses provided default
- useEffect syncs with localStorage after hydration
- Prevents hydration mismatch errors

**Location:** apps/web/src/hooks/use-local-storage.ts

**Reasoning:** Many features need persistent state (basket, theme, variable inputs). Centralizing localStorage logic in a hook ensures consistent behavior and SSR safety.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-10T00:34:35.714509321-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-10T01:44:46.164609393-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-10T01:44:46.164609393-05:00","close_reason":"Added apps/web/src/hooks/use-local-storage.ts SSR-safe localStorage hook"}
 {"id":"jeffreysprompts.com-ro6f","title":"Build content reporting flow","description":"## Origin
Moved from jeffreysprompts_premium jeffreysprompts_premium-xlo.

## Task
Build content reporting flow for community moderation.

## User Flow

### Report Button
- Flag icon on all public content
- Tooltip: "Report this content"
- Only visible to logged-in users
- Hidden on own content

### Report Dialog
On click, opens modal:

1. **Reason Selection** (required)
   - Spam or misleading content
   - Inappropriate or offensive
   - Copyright violation
   - Contains harmful content
   - Other

2. **Details** (optional)
   - Textarea for additional context
   - Max 500 characters

3. **Submit**
   - "Report" button
   - Loading state
   - Success toast: "Report submitted. We'll review within 24 hours."

### Database Schema
```typescript
export const contentReports = pgTable("content_reports", {
  id: uuid("id").primaryKey().defaultRandom(),
  reporterId: uuid("reporter_id").notNull().references(() => users.id),
  contentType: text("content_type").notNull(),
  contentId: uuid("content_id").notNull(),
  reason: text("reason").notNull(),
  details: text("details"),
  status: text("status", { 
    enum: ["pending", "reviewed", "actioned", "dismissed"] 
  }).default("pending"),
  reviewedBy: uuid("reviewed_by"),
  reviewedAt: timestamp("reviewed_at"),
  action: text("action"),  // warn, remove, ban
  adminNotes: text("admin_notes"),
  createdAt: timestamp("created_at").defaultNow(),
}, (table) => [
  index("idx_reports_status").on(table.status),
  index("idx_reports_content").on(table.contentType, table.contentId),
]);
```

### API Endpoint
POST /api/reports
Request: { contentType, contentId, reason, details }
Response: { success: true }

### Rate Limiting
- Max 10 reports per user per day
- Prevent report spam
